channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Senate confirms Noem as Trump's homeland security secretary
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate confirmed Kristi Noem as homeland security secretary on Saturday, putting the South Dakota governor in charge of a sprawling agency that is essential to national security and President Donald Trump's plans to clamp down on illegal immigration.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump reverses Biden's ban on private prison contracts
item.description: Trump's action comes after signals from private prison corporations like GEO Group and CoreCivic that they expected such a reversal.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Devastating toll for Gaza's children: Over 13,000 killed and an estimated 25,000 injured, UN says
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The war in Gaza has been devastating for children: More than 13,000 have been killed, an estimated 25,000 injured, and at least 25,000 hospitalized for malnutrition, according to U.N. agencies. As Britain’s deputy U.N.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Private investigator first to admit guilt in offering bribes to get intoxicated drivers off the hook
item.description: A private investigator embroiled in a corruption scandal that rocked New Mexico’s law enforcement community for over a decade was the first to plead guilty to a slew of federal charges that include racketeering and bribery, according to court records released Friday.
item.dc:creator: SEJAL GOVINDARAO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: More DEI fallout: Air Force scraps course that used videos of Tuskegee Airmen and female WWII pilots
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Air Force has removed training courses with videos of its storied Tuskegee Airmen and the Women Airforce Service Pilots, or WASPs — the female World War II pilots who were vital in ferrying warplanes for the military — to comply with the Trump administration's crackdown on dive
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump touts 'no-tax-on-tips' plan in Nevada victory lap
item.description: When Nevada flipped red in November, Trump became the first GOP presidential candidate to win the key swing state since President George W. Bush in 2004.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Washington state woman is charged in the fatal shooting of a Border Patrol agent in Vermont
item.description: A Washington state woman has been charged in the fatal shooting of a U.S. Border Patrol agent in Vermont, which happened days after authorities began watching her and a German companion who also died in the roadside shootout, the FBI said Friday.
item.dc:creator: HOLLY RAMER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 4 tips for launching a side hustle at any age
item.description: Either start your own business or join an existing side-hustle platform that matches people with opportunities.
item.dc:creator: Kimberly Palmer, KRON4

item.title: Ozempic and similar weight loss drugs may lower risk of 42 health conditions, but also pose risks
item.description: The Food and Drug Administration’s approval of Ozempic in 2021 for weight loss treatment ushered in a new era for the class of drugs called glucagon-like peptide-1 agonists, or GLP-1.
item.dc:creator: Ziyad Al-Aly Clinical Epidemiologist, Washington University in St. Louis, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Oklahoma bill would make it a crime punishable by jail time to "possess" a shopping cart belonging to a business
item.description: An Oklahoma lawmaker is proposing a law that would make it a crime punishable by up to a year in jail for anyone caught “in possession of” a shopping cart taken from a store.
item.dc:creator: Spencer Humphrey/KFOR, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Former NBA star marks two years of his cannabis shop in St. Louis
item.description: There was celebration and appreciation in downtown St. Louis Friday as former NBA star Al Harrington marked the two-year anniversary of Viola Cannabis Dispensary.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Harris, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: WATCH: Nuns have snowball fight during winter storm
item.description: Several nuns duked it out last week, having a snowball fight during a winter storm that swept across the Deep South.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Ohio school bus driver rescues family's pets from burning home
item.description: Bitter cold temperatures closed schools in a Stark County community on Wednesday, so it was a day off for Todd Morris as well.
item.dc:creator: Dave Nethers, KRON4

item.title: Live updates: Trump visits Las Vegas after whirlwind first days in office
item.description: President Trump is in Las Vegas Saturday after a whirlwind first few days in the Oval Office, filled with executive orders and travel across the country as his agenda takes form in Washington.
item.dc:creator: The Hill Staff, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mexico refuses Trump admin. migrant deportation flights, sources say
item.description: U.S. military aircraft carried out two similar flights, each with about 80 migrants, to Guatemala on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: As schools in LA reopen, parents worry about harmful ash from wildfires
item.description: Weeks after the worst of the deadly fires tore through Los Angeles suburbs, families are grappling with the reality that toxic ash from burned cars and homes could be a health issue for awhile, especially for children.
item.dc:creator: Dorany Pineda And Brittany Peterson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Man says he was behind some of the viral googly eyes on public art in Oregon
item.description: PORTLAND, Ore. (AP) — A resident of the central Oregon city of Bend says he was the person behind some of the googly eyes that appeared on sculptures around the city in recent months and sparked a viral sensation widely covered by news outlets.
item.dc:creator: CLAIRE RUSH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump pumps coal as answer to AI power needs but any boost could be short-lived
item.description: CHEYENNE, Wyo. (AP) — President Donald Trump’s promise to go all in on fossil fuels includes praise for coal, a reliable but polluting energy source that's long been in decline.
item.dc:creator: MEAD GRUVER and MATTHEW BROWN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: SZA to join Kendrick Lamar for Super Bowl LIX halftime show
item.description: Apple Music released a teaser trailer on Thursday showing the Compton native on the gridiron, assessing the field before the big game while he's talking to someone on the phone.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Brawl over Pokémon cards breaks out at California Costco
item.description: The fight, captured on video by X user DisguisedToast, unfolded on Jan. 16 at the Costco in Atwater Village, the Los Angeles Times reported.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, Fox 5

item.title: Secret Service says it visited Chicago elementary school, not ICE
item.description: Chicago Public Schools officials said in a press conference Friday that Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) agents showed up at an elementary school in the city but were denied entry. But later on Friday, the Secret Service said it visited the school, not ICE.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Vance tells March for Life it's a 'blessing to fight for the unborn' during first week in office
item.description: JD Vance told the March for Life crowd Friday he wants to bring back a focus on families in the United States with President's Trump's new administration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Florida man pleads guilty to assaulting American Airlines gate agent
item.description: A Florida man pleaded guilty in federal court after he assaulted an American Airlines gate agent at Dallas-Fort Worth International Airport, according to online court documents.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Accettulla, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Idaho beefs up firing squad as Bryan Kohbeger trial nears
item.description: A new bill would make the firing squad Idaho's primary means of execution, after the state recently restored it as a backup amid struggles with lethal injection.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Costco stands by its DEI policies as other companies scale back
item.description: Costco is pushing back on a shareholder proposal that urges the wholesale club operator to conduct an evaluation of any business risks posed by its diversity, equity and inclusion practices. Investors were expected to vote on the recommendation during the company's annual meeting Thursday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump cites voter ID, water flow as conditions for LA wildfire aid
item.description: President Donald Trump on Friday said he wanted to see two actions taken in California before he offered federal support for Los Angeles as it grapples with wildfires.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Target latest company to roll back DEI programs
item.description: Target has announced it is rolling back its diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) policies, becoming the latest corporation to do so following President Donald Trump’s election.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Spirit Airlines updates dress code, tattoo policy for passengers
item.description: Passengers flying on Spirit Airlines can now be removed from a flight if a tattoo or other "body art" is deemed "lewd, obscene, or offensive.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Accettulla, Fox 5

item.title: ICE agents raid New Jersey seafood store, detain US military veteran
item.description: The store owner told NewsNation affiliate WPIX that a U.S. military veteran was among the people detained.
item.dc:creator: Ben Mitchell, NewsNation

item.title: 80 Guatemalan migrants deported from Biggs Army Airfield
item.description: EL PASO, Texas (KTSM) — The U.S. Department of Defense, along with U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE), deported 80 Guatemalan migrants on Friday morning, Jan. 24, from Biggs Army Airfield at Fort Bliss, U.S. Rep. Tony Gonzales, R-Texas said in a post on X.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Luna, NewsNation

item.title: Trump proposes 'getting rid of FEMA' during North Carolina visit
item.description: President Donald Trump said he was considering "getting rid of FEMA” as he visited hurricane-battered North Carolina on Friday during the first trip of his second term.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and CHRIS MEGERIAN Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: JetBlue sued after 'watermelon' sized ice block drops through roof
item.description: A couple is suing a major U.S. airline after a block of ice came crashing through the roof of their home last year.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, NewsNation

item.title: Ford recalls over 270,000 Broncos and Mavericks: Here's why
item.description: Ford Motor Co. is recalling 272,827 Broncos and Mavericks due to a power problem that can cause vehicles to stop unexpectedly or fail to start.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: ICE agents raid NJ seafood store, detain US military veteran
item.description: "I asked them [the agents] what documentation they were looking for, and they said it was a license or a passport.
item.dc:creator: Ben Mitchell, WGN 9

item.title: Snowmen replace sandcastles on Alabama beach after historic winter storm
item.description: Many people who don't normally get to experience snow found unique ways to enjoy the rare sight this week as a historic winter storm moved across the South.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump signs order directing release of JFK assassination files
item.description: The order directs the director of national intelligence and attorney general to present a plan within 15 days for the “full and complete release of records relating to the assassination of President John F. Kennedy.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, ABC 4

item.title: Disneyland may remove longtime Haunted Mansion element, Imagineer says
item.description: The creative team at Disneyland has considered removing one of the Haunted Mansion’s many “frightful delights,” according to an Imagineer.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5
